title: Power outages add to Kochi’s summer woes
content: Power outages hit key areas, including Vyttila, Kadavanthra, Kalamassery, and Thripunithura. KSEB officials blame excess power consumption during the scorching summer for frequent night-time supply failures

title: SFI seeks action against faculty members accused of harassing medical PG students
content: All 12 PG doctors in the department have lodged a complaint with the Principal, alleging humiliation by two faculty members

title: Rodents destroy currency notes seized in corruption case, Supreme Court expresses surprise
content: The court said the explanation offered for the destruction of currency notes does not inspire any confidence

title: Shuttered filling stations, restricted sales fuel anxiety in Andhra Pradesh
content: Though the State government and oil marketing companies maintain that there is no scarcity of diesel and petrol, serpentine queues have become a common sight at outlets in Rajahmundry, Eluru, Vijayawada, Tirupati, Guntur, Kakinada, and Kurnool

title: FYUGP students in Calicut varsity allowed do internship in their own colleges this year
content: The new order allows students to undertake internship at their own colleges. Teachers will be permitted to oversee the work and evaluate their performance

title: A.B. Venkateswara Rao issues legal notices to retired and serving IPS officers
content: The notices are for damages amounting to ₹1 crore, compensation for malicious prosecution, unlawful suspension, fabrication of official records, defamation, and violation of fundamental rights guaranteed under Article 21 of the Constitution, he says

title: Kidwai’s doorstep palliative care sees steady uptake; no-cost services reach 112 patients
content: The service currently covers Bengaluru within a radius of about 40 km from the institute and is delivered through two dedicated teams conducting daily home visits

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary continues courtesy visits to Nitish Kumar’s residence
content: In the last 10 days, Mr. Choudhary has made courtesy visits to Mr. Kumar’s residence four times, taking time out of his busy schedule. The former CM continues to stay in the bungalow designated for the CM.

title: Civil Services still relevant, says former Kerala Chief Secretary K. Jayakumar
content: Former Chief Secretary inaugurates the two-day The Hindu Civil Service Gateway 2026 in Thiruvananthapuram

title: Bengaluru Metro non-fare revenue surges from ₹54 crore to ₹145 crore
content: The BMRCL began generating non-fare revenue as early as 2012-13, but the scale and diversity of income streams have expanded significantly in recent years

title: Interview | BJP will form govt. on its own strength this time in Bengal, says senior leader Tapas Roy
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee’s one-point agenda is to remain in power, and for that she is willing to do anything, claims former Minister Tapas Roy, now candidate from Maniktala seat in north Kolkata.

title: AAP stares at a weakened road to 2027 Punjab Assembly election
content: The timing of the AAP MPs’ exit from the party is as damaging as the defection itself; the party’s bid gain sympathy by branding the defectors as traitors may offer little electoral consolation

title: Anakapalli to get Godavari water through Polavaram Left Main Canal by June, says A.P. Minister
content: More than 28,500 acres and 110 minor irrigation tanks will get access to irrigation water and 143 villages covering 5.23 lakh population will get safe drinking water, says Ramanaidu

title: Use knowledge for progress, be job creators: Vice-President to youth
content: Radhakrishnan stresses innovation, ethical conduct and women’s empowerment at Rajasthan University convocation

title: Rumours of rift in Congress over CM nominee in Kerala a ‘media creation’ says IUML
content: Kunhalikutty says if the UDF is voted to power, the Chief Minister would be decided by the Congress keeping in mind the ‘people’s sentiments.’

title: Muni Narayana Prasad, scholar and head of Narayana Gurukula, passes away
content: A highly respected figure in the lineage of Sree Narayana Guru, he had headed the Narayana Gurukula for the past 27 years

title: ‘Space City’ vision of A.P. is taking shape, says Minister Nara Lokesh
content: Astrobase is setting up India’s first private 200-ton thrust cryogenic engine test facility in Thottambedu mandal of Tirupati district, says the IT Minister

title: Kavitha launches Telangana Rashtra Sena, charges BRS with betraying Telangana
content: The former BRS MLC also apologised to the people for being part of a family that had ‘misruled’ the State for nearly a decade since Telangana’s formation

title: Dalit murder case: YSRCP MLC Ananta Udaya Bhaskar sent to 14-day judicial custody
content: Udaya Bhaskar was taken into custody for intimidating four witnesses in the 2022 Dalit youth murder case and violating the bail conditions of the Supreme Court, say police

title: Kochi NGO Maithri receives distress calls via AI referrals
content: Calls referred by artificial intelligence-powered applications are now being directed to Maithri, says its director. Daily calls have risen to an average of 10, leading to a call for more volunteers

title: Kurnool Collector assures no fuel shortage, urges public against hoarding
content: Addressing the media on the fuel stocks and related issues, A.Siri says that a total of 166 petrol bunks are currently operational in the district

title: A.P. Ministers study city design, infra planning in Singapore
content: The delegation gains insights into Singapore’s planning strategies such as ‘scenario planning’ and ‘urban diplomacy’

title: No restriction on women entering mosques, AIMPLB tells Supreme Court
content: Islamic practice does not bar women from entering mosques, though their participation in congregational prayers is not mandatory, says senior advocate M.R. Shamshad

title: Central Travancore wilts under heat, water sources run dry
content: Wells are rapidly receding, while streams and other natural water sources have remained dry for months

title: Three counting centres for 12 Assembly constituencies in Palakkad
content: Counting of votes will be held at Government Victoria College, Palakkad; NSS Engineering College, Akathethara; and Mount Seena Public School, Pathiripala

title: ‘Punjab de Gaddar’: AAP holds protests outside residences of MPs who quit to join BJP
content: AAP Punjab Youth Wing acting president Parminder Goldy led a protest outside the residence of MP Rajinder Gupta in Ludhiana, raising slogans against him

title: Antivenom administration: Kerala Government Medical Officers’ Association demands adequate HR and better infrastructure in hospitals
content: Public must be educated about the serious practical challenges and safety concerns that arise when a snakebite victim has to be administered the anti-snake venom, says KGMOA

title: Watch: Gunfire, blasts rock Mali's capital; India issues advisory
content: Fresh violence erupted across Mali early Saturday, with "unidentified armed terrorist groups" targeting multiple locations including the capital Bamako, its international airport, and the key military town of Kati. Attacks also spread to northern cities, with the Azawad Liberation Front reportedly entering Kidal. The defense minister's house in Kati was reportedly hit.

title: 'Torn pants to Rs 100 cr house': Swati Maliwal's 'traitor' jibe at Kejriwal after AAP exit
content: Former Arvind Kejriwal aide Swati Maliwal has accused the former Delhi chief minister. She claims Kejriwal began his political journey in torn pants and with limited resources. Maliwal alleges that after coming to power, he constructed a house valued at one hundred crore rupees. She also criticized the AAP's governance in Punjab. The remarks follow a significant party exodus.

title: Microsoft’s former CEO says “I feel silly,” shares letter to judge over fintech loss
content: Former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer is furious after losing $60 million to fintech founder Joseph Sanberg's Aspiration Partners. Sanberg defrauded investors with false environmental claims. Ballmer's reputation suffered, leading to a public campaign against him and an NBA investigation. Sanberg's plea deal and sentencing are pending.

title: Watch: Epstein files video showing Trump projected on to building in Washington DC
content: A provocative protest projected an old video of Donald Trump with Jeffrey Epstein onto the Washington Hilton, the venue for the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The display highlighted Trump's past association with the disgraced financier and criticised the administration's handling of the Epstein files, with messages suggesting complicity and a cover-up.

title: AAP implosion: BJP gains RS numbers, but will it be advantage Congress in Punjab?
content: The AAP, as expected, reacted very strongly to this development and accused the BJP of “Operation Lotus”, a term used to define saffron party engineered defections of political rivals. AAP leader Sanjay Singh alleged that central agencies like the Enforcement Directorate (ED) and the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) were misused to pressure and break away AAP leaders. AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal accused the BJP of betraying Punjab, which is ruled by AAP and goes to assembly polls next year.

title: Record shattered! Punjab Kings chase 264 vs Delhi Capitals - here are IPL’s highest successful run chases
content: Punjab Kings achieved the highest successful run chase in IPL history, chasing down a colossal 264 against Delhi Capitals. Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh provided a blistering start, followed by a match-winning unbeaten 71 from captain Shreyas Iyer, securing a six-wicket victory with seven balls to spare.

title: KPMG to lay off 10% of US audit team after failed retirement efforts
content: KPMG is set to cut approximately 10% of its US audit partners after voluntary retirement schemes failed to yield desired results. The accounting giant, one of the Big Four, cited a need to align its partnership size with current business demands.

title: Watch: China navy's video hints its 4th aircraft carrier will be nuclear powered
content: China's potential fourth aircraft carrier, possibly named 'He Jian' and hinted to be nuclear-powered, signals a significant advancement in its naval capabilities. This development, showcased in the film 'Into The Deep', aligns with China's transition to a blue-water navy and its expanding global maritime ambitions.

title: 3 dead, 17 houses burnt in 24 hrs, fresh clashes: Why Manipur is on the boil again
content: Protests erupted in Manipur as thousands demanded peace, clashing with security forces near the chief minister's residence. Demonstrators, organised by COCOMI, sought justice for children killed in a recent bomb blast and support for the displaced. The state grapples with escalating ethnic violence, with recent clashes claiming lives and displacing many.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office day 38: Film slowly inches towards Rs 1800 cr worldwide
content: In a stunning display of cinematic prowess, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' has captivated audiences, amassing nearly Rs 1800 crore globally. Surpassing the Rs 1700 crore mark, it now stands as the third highest-grossing Indian film. By its sixth week, it continues to fend off stiff competition, raking in remarkable box office figures both at home and abroad. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: Drill, sanction, control: Inside the oil economics driving Trump 2.0
content: US President Trump's second term prioritizes oil, leveraging energy dominance for global influence. Campaign funding from oil interests aligns with policies boosting domestic production and easing regulations. Sanctions on rivals and geopolitical events like the Hormuz crisis engineer demand for American exports, impacting nations like India and reshaping global energy markets.

title: Scary scenes! Lungi Ngidi taken off in ambulance after suffering head injury
content: The incident happened in the third over of Punjab Kings' chase when Arya played a slash at a full ball on the stumps off Axar Patel. The ball went high towards mid-off, where Ngidi ran back to complete the catch.

title: Ronaldo’s 3 final career goals before retirement – Messi already ahead
content: Cristiano Ronaldo is entering the final phase of his career with three clear goals still driving him forward: a first World Cup title in 2026, the pursuit of 1,000 career goals, and the rare chance of playing alongside his son at Al Nassr. Even at 41, he remains central for club and country, shaping both legacy and milestones.

title: Patrick Mahomes & Brittany Mahomes hit $325m valuation amid recovery
content: Patrick Mahomes, the Kansas City Chiefs’ quarterback, is focused on his recovery from his brutal injuries as his wife, Brittany Mahomes stands by his side through all the difficult times. While both Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, have preferred to be private about the star player’s recovery, the high profile couple recently received an update worth a whopping $325 million.

title: Titans uncover affair? Mike Vrabel exit linked to Russini drama
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has taken some time off from work to be with his family as his alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini continue to get worse every day. Though the New England Patriots have not yet hinted at firing Mike Vrabel ahead of the new season, it seems his time with the Tennessee Titans is being viewed differently.

title: Raghav Chadha loses over 1m followers, flooded with hate comments after joining BJP
content: Raghav Chadha's Instagram following plummeted by over 1 million after his switch from AAP to BJP. Once a youth icon, his digital persona faced backlash, with followers expressing disappointment and betrayal. Allegations of deleting critical posts against the BJP surfaced, which Chadha denied. His appeal previously rested on addressing everyday issues, a stark contrast to his current political move.

title: NFL trade rumor: Falcons eye $63M ex-patriots star despite issues
content: The Atlanta Falcons have failed to perform well in the last couple of seasons as it has missed qualifying in the playoffs for the eighth time in a row, sparking concern among fans about it. With just 8 wins and 9 losses in the last two consecutive seasons, all focus is on bouncing back in the new season.

title: 'Gunfire everywhere': Explosions heard near Mali international airport, military base
content: Mali faced coordinated attacks on Saturday. Gunfire and explosions were heard in the capital Bamako and near a military camp housing Russian mercenaries. Unidentified terrorist groups targeted several positions. Fighting continued in the capital and other regions. Security forces responded to the unfolding situation. Al-Qaeda affiliate JNIM is suspected of involvement.

title: Handwritten letters, human chain: How Mojtaba Khamenei controls Iran from the shadows
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is reportedly leading the country through a secret "human chain" of handwritten letters to evade US and Israeli detection.  This comes amid unprecedented security measures, with senior officials avoiding visits due to assassination fears following a reported US-Israel strike that gravely injured him.

title: IPL: KL Rahul scripts history, becomes first Indian to…
content: KL Rahul achieved a historic milestone with an unbeaten 152 for Delhi Capitals against Punjab Kings. This remarkable innings is the third-highest individual score in IPL history. Rahul's performance, aided by dropped catches, powered Delhi to a commanding total. He partnered with Nitish Rana for a significant second-wicket stand, setting a new franchise record.

title: IPL 2026: KL Rahul creates IPL history with unique 3-franchise century feat
content: KL Rahul unleashed a sensational unbeaten 152, a record for an Indian in the IPL, as Delhi Capitals posted a colossal 264/2 against Punjab Kings. Supported by Nitish Rana's 91, their 220-run partnership powered DC to their highest-ever total. Rahul's fiery knock featured 16 fours and nine sixes, marking his fastest century.

title: 'Left out of disgust, not fear': Raghav Chadha attacks AAP over Sheesh Mahal 2.0
content: Raghav Chadha, a prominent AAP leader, has quit the party citing disappointment and disgust, not fear, over alleged corruption and a "Sheesh Mahal" controversy. He stated that seven MPs are leaving together, believing the AAP has fallen into compromised hands. Chadha's departure follows weeks of escalating tensions and his removal as deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha.

title: What's cooking in pantry? Bite into 'adulterated' truth
content: Food adulteration in India poses a significant public health crisis, with contaminated milk, paneer, and processed foods causing severe health issues and even deaths. Recent incidents highlight widespread violations, including a deadly milk contamination in Andhra Pradesh and large seizures of adulterated paneer in Surat, underscoring the urgent need for stricter regulations and enforcement.

title: IPL: KL Rahul, Nitish Rana break all-time record vs Punjab Kings
content: KL Rahul and Nitish Rana delivered a batting spectacle, forging a record 150+ run partnership for Delhi Capitals against Punjab Kings. Rahul's explosive century, coupled with Rana's rapid half-century and a massive 28-run over, powered DC to a commanding position. This dominant display rewrote DC's record books.

title: Iran war could push over 32m people into poverty; UN warns of 'triple shock'
content: A United Nations report warns the Iran war's economic fallout could plunge over 32 million people into poverty globally. Developing nations face the brunt of this crisis, grappling with surging energy and food prices. The conflict reverses development gains, pushing vulnerable populations back into destitution. Urgent global support is needed to aid these hardest-hit countries and prevent lasting damage.

title: IPL: KL Rahul goes berserk with 47-ball ton, equals Gayle to enter elite list
content: KL Rahul smashed a brilliant IPL century, his sixth, off just 47 balls against Punjab Kings.  Capitalizing on two dropped catches, Rahul, supported by Nitish Rana's unbeaten 72, forged a record 185-run partnership. This landmark knock places Rahul among IPL's top centurions and highlights his unique achievement of scoring centuries for three different franchises.

title: AAP crisis: Punjab CM seeks Prez's intervention against 7 MPs who joined BJP
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann will meet President Murmu to seek the recall of seven AAP MPs, including six from Punjab, who have joined the BJP. This move follows Raghav Chadha's announcement that two-thirds of AAP's Rajya Sabha members are merging with the BJP, citing the party's deviation from its principles.

title: Shashank's costly drop of KL Rahul leaves Ponting fuming, Preity Zinta stunned
content: Shashank Singh dropped a crucial catch of KL Rahul off Arshdeep Singh's bowling in the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Punjab Kings. The Punjab fielder, who had a similar lapse in the previous match, grassed a sitter at deep square leg. Rahul, on 12, went on to capitalize on the reprieve.

title: 'Beaten up, threatened...': After joining BJP, Maliwal recalls 2024 assault at Kejriwal's house
content: Rajya Sabha MP Swati Maliwal, now with the BJP, revealed she was intimidated and threatened after alleging an assault by an AAP aide at Kejriwal's residence in 2024. She claimed immense pressure to withdraw her FIR, leading to her being sidelined by the party. Maliwal also criticized AAP's governance in Punjab and praised PM Modi's leadership.

title: ‘Made Rs 80L to 1Cr’ off slapgate: Sreesanth ends all ties with Harbhajan
content: S Sreesanth has cut ties with Harbhajan Singh after the IPL slapgate row resurfaced. Upset over an ad linked to the incident, he said, "I'll forgive but I'll never forget." Sreesanth added, "I have no relationship with that person," blocked him on social media, and called his public image “an act”.

title: YouTuber, who changed identity, arrested for killing boy 31 years ago
content: A prominent YouTuber and social activist, Salim Wastik, has been arrested by Delhi Police for a 1995 kidnapping and murder. Previously known as Salim Khan, he had been on the run for 26 years after escaping interim bail.  His newfound fame as a vocal critic of Islam ultimately led to his capture, bringing a decades-old cold case to a close.

title: 'This is where I studied': MP board topper Chandani's parents say there's no one like her
content: Chandni Vishwakarma, hailing from Bhopal's Bhim Nagar, exemplifies resilience as she emerged as the 12th board topper with an impressive 98.8% in commerce. Living in a modest one-room home with her father, a carpenter, and her mother, who serves in a mid-day meal program, she navigated through the challenges of limited resources and constant noise.

title: 'Their fault': Anna Hazare holds AAP responsible for Raghav Chadha, 6 others joining BJP
content: Veteran activist Anna Hazare criticized AAP leadership following the departure of Raghav Chadha and six other Rajya Sabha MPs, suggesting their exit stemmed from the party deviating from its original principles. Chadha announced the group's decision to merge with the BJP, citing constitutional provisions and claiming a significant portion of AAP's Rajya Sabha members had left.

title: 'Delhi Capitals making silly mistakes': Axar Patel's blunt admission during toss
content: Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel won the toss and chose to bat against table-toppers Punjab Kings. Patel acknowledged his team's avoidable mistakes, aiming to post a strong total and defend it on a dry, hot pitch. Both sides fielded unchanged XIs, with Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer emphasizing staying in the moment and continuous improvement.

title: Shocker for India's solar sector: US slaps 123% anti-dumping duty on imports
content: The US has slapped a hefty 123.04% anti-dumping duty on Indian solar cells and modules, citing "critical circumstances." This, combined with existing duties, makes US shipments unviable. However, Indian solar firms have already diversified exports to Europe and West Asia, mitigating the immediate impact. The industry plans to challenge the "flawed" findings.

title: Raghav Chadha-led exodus jolts AAP; party races to recalibrate strategy: What next?
content: Seven Aam Aadmi Party MPs, including Raghav Chadha, defected to the Bharatiya Janata Party, citing a deviation from the party's core principles. The move is permissible under the anti-defection law as it involves more than two-thirds of the AAP's Rajya Sabha members. AAP plans to seek disqualification for some MPs.

title: PCB chief Mohsin Naqvi confirms spectators for PSL 2026 final in Lahore
content: Pakistan Cricket Board chairman Mohsin Naqvi confirmed that fans will be allowed to attend the Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2026 final in Lahore on May 3, following approval from Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif. This decision comes after earlier matches were played behind closed doors due to fuel price concerns and government-advised austerity measures.

title: BJP shares 'Sheeshmahal 2' pics; AAP calls them 'fake', asks Delhi CM to show her house
content: BJP has sharply criticised Arvind Kejriwal, labelling him the "Rahman Dakait of Delhi" and accusing him of lavish living. BJP leader Parvesh Verma alleged Kejriwal built a "Sheesh Mahal 2" while Delhi suffered during the Covid wave and has now moved into a luxurious bungalow, abandoning his image of simplicity.

title: 'Only revealed Sangh’s true nature': Rahul targets RSS after Ram Madhav’s Russia-Iran oil remarks
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi seized upon remarks by RSS leader Ram Madhav to criticise Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Madhav's initial comments suggested India yielded to US pressure on oil imports and tariffs, which he later retracted and apologized for. Congress claims this indicates PM Modi has lost his authority and India's sovereignty is compromised.

title: AAP Rebel Met Arvind Kejriwal Before Raghav Chadha Shocker. What He Told Him
content: Vikramjit Singh Sahney revealed that he met with AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal on Wednesday and informed him about his plan to join the BJP.

title: Beijing Warns Europe As Chinese Entities Included In Russia Sanctions
content: The European Union sanctions package on Russia targets third-country suppliers of critical high-tech items, including China-based entities

title: 'Economic Growth Disconnected From Poll Outcomes': Ruchir Sharma On Bengal Election
content: "Even with 8 per cent economic growth, the chances of a chief minister being re-elected was 50-50, not that different from what the natural average is anyway," Ruchir Sharma said

title: FBI Chief Kash Patel Was Arrested For Public Drinking, Urination: Report
content: FBI Director Kash Patel was celebrating with his friends that night and further wrote in the letter that "we attempted to relieve our bladders while walking home". Patel added that before they could continue the act, a police cruiser stopped them, after which they were arrested for public urination.

title: Many Injured In Clashes At Imphal Rally Called Over Recent Attacks
content: COCOMI said the rally was aimed at seeking answers from the government on key issues, including alleged "proxy war" and narco-terrorism

title: US Modifies Sanctions, Allows Venezuela To Pay For Nicolas Maduro's Lawyer
content: Maduro's lawyer had earlier asked a US District judge to dismiss the case because US sanctions were preventing Venezuela from paying his legal fees.

title: Immigration Arrests In US Down By Nearly 12% After Minneapolis Killings
content: In December, arrests by Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents peaked at nearly 40,000 nationwide and were nearly as high the next month.

title: Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Somalia Coast, Taken Into Its Territorial Waters
content: The development added to risks for navigating in the Red Sea, which has become a more important supply route given the Iranian stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz at the entrance to the Gulf, on the other side of the Arabian Peninsula.

title: AAP vs BJP Over 'Sheesh Mahal 2'. Raghav Chadha Weighs In
content: Parvesh Verma released images of Arvind Kejriwal's new bungalow, alleging that "private money" was used for the superior quality amenities in that house.

title: NDTV Yuva 2026 LIVE: Avinash Tiwary On His Journey To Bollywood And Being An 'Outsider'
content: NDTV Yuva 2026: The event will feature several key speakers who have left their mark in politics, sports, entertainment, and social impact.

title: Ukrainian Drone Hits Building In Russian City 1,900 Kms Away From Kyiv
content: There were no fatalities but some people suffered minor injuries and one woman was hospitalised, the governor of the Sverdlovsk region said.

title: WFH To Limited AC Use: How Countries Are Dealing With Iran War Energy Shock
content: Countries across Asia and as far as Argentina have begun introducing measures to manage supply pressures and shield consumers from rising fuel costs.

title: Watch: Trump-Epstein Videos Projected Onto Washington DC Building
content: The visuals included photographs and documents connected to late paedophile Jeffrey Epstein, accompanied by audio that reportedly featured readings of his emails. A crowd gathered across the street, watching as the projections unfolded on the buildin

title: In A First, Press-Critic Trump To Attend Media Event On Saturday
content: After Trump boycotted the event in his first term and in 2025, his participation now has become both the subject of surprise and high anticipation

title: Meet Nitu Samra, Interim CEO Steering Noida Airport Through Clearance Hurdle
content: Nitu Samra's appointment follows the exit of founding CEO Christoph Schnellmann, after he was denied mandatory security clearance.

title: Elderly Man Fed Spiked Lassi And Robbed In Gujarat, Woman Arrested
content: Lataben gained entry into the home of Abdulbhai Ismailbhai Sipai and his wife in the Dariyapur locality by offering assistance with household chores and meal preparation.

title: Will Raghav Chadha Move Impact Punjab? AAP, BJP Face Off, Protests Erupt
content: Protests erupted outside the residences of the Rajya Sabha MPs from Punjab who defected to the BJP, including that of former cricketer Harbhajan Singh.

title: Trump To Host Crypto Contest Winners As $TRUMP Coin Plunges 96% From Peak
content: When the contest closed earlier this month, $TRUMP closed at $2.81, down steeply from the $75 all-time high

title: Man's Hands Cut With Grinder Over Payment Dispute In Delhi: Cops
content: A 32-year-old man was seriously injured after an alleged brutal assault over a payment dispute in Vijay Enclave area of Dwarka in the national capital, police said on Saturday.
